item.title: Trying to unmake a hero...
item.description: I work in televised news. We just aired a network story where the reported really hammered on how rich Luigi is. How incredibly privileged he is. How he was raised in a town where his family owns two country clubs, how he went to a private school, and went so far as to point out his distant cousin who is a government statesman. Just casually mentioning all of this, using the word 'privileged' several times. It's just an effort to dampen his popularity. "You don't want to cheer him, do you? He's so very rich and privileged and wealthy and did we mention privileged?" Don't fall for the propaganda.
item.dc:creator: helljack

item.dc:creator: deafkitten

item.title: CEO Wanted Posters have gone up all across Manhattan
item.description: In addition to pictures and names of several health insurance CEOs, the posters read, "Healthcare CEOs should not feel safe. UnitedHealthcare killed everyday people for the sake of profit. As a result Brian Thompson was denied his claim to life. Who will be denied next?"
item.dc:creator: Gorglax9

item.title: I see you know your Judo well
item.dc:creator: maamby

item.title: America's Oligarchy
item.description: FP Edit: Morning folks. I'm not having too good of a day myself (still recovering from my seizure the other day.) -Regarding this ruling. This sets it so other third parties can have more time to secure funds, and out/bid/maneuver the current winner The Onion. This does not bode well for anybody who wants to see less of Alex Jones and his Cesspool of a platform. Also, keep in mind the future ruling will happen under Trump. This is not just about getting the Sandy Hook victims more money. It is about stopping Alex Jones from keeping hold of his platform. By doing so we can prevent more victims like the ones from Sandy Hook that he harassed. This is not a hard concept. Could you do me a favor? If you are on BlueSky or YouTube I'd appreciate a follow and subscribe. Jokes aside I hope to see you all there BlueSky- @gridsquares.bsky.social Youtube- @VeteranGridSquares -Original Post- Can someone please tell me why we are following the rule of law anymore? Judges do whatever they want, they don't care about precedent or law. The only thing that seems to rule is the holy dollar. Folks I'm so fucking tired of the rich getting away with the literal murder. If anybody wondered why people were rooting against the CEO, this is exactly why. If you have money, you can get the law to work in your favor. Folks let me know what you think of the comments below.
item.dc:creator: VeteranGridSquares

item.dc:creator: DigitalPoyo

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1210 (brainfog)
item.description:
Bread. Whatever. I don't care.
I'm so tired of this timeline. No revolution, no Onion infowars. Just the boot. The boot, and the whip.
The Marketplace gives me no tax credit this year. So I'm going to have to pay 600 a month for insurance. The ACA Health plan I was on for some reason kicked me off of it. God fucking bless this shithole of a Protestant soul-sucking murderous octegenarian kaikostracy. The Line must go up so the CEOs and Shareholders can have their yachts and safety bunkers.
I make, on average, $2000 a month in a good month. Slightly over Half goes directly into rent. Then food, life insurance, gas for travel, etc. If I'm lucky, I get about $200-$400 left to play with, in a good month. $ I've cut all streaming services except Paramont from my life. About the only way I can save more is if I cancel my pet insurance for Pippin, and drastically decrease my food budget and subsist on rice and Ramen alone. No meat, no dairy, no healthy foods. I can't exactly skimp on Pippin in terms of his food and toys, that would be like depriving a child. Also, i guess cutting out all hobbies such as my mead making. That would probably give me an extra 130 a month I guess. Have to kiss saving up for the new computer and bronze-age sword goodbye. This is America. If it doesn't make the line go up and the CEOs and Oligarchs yacht money, it doesn't matter.
I mean, at least now I know there are... other options regarding insurance. For legal reasons, this is a joke.
Really feeling like Achilles right now. A ball of range and anger.
The meal, for some reason, is also $40.
Fam, I'm already failing the week challenge, spam F in the chat for your homie.
This would still have been a better timeline. I think I need to remember to live with the fact we are in the worst possible timeline, the worst possible universe. And if this is a simulation, buddy, I am going to find the plug and pull it so hard that we all see stars.
People do approach me randomly in the liquor isles.
Yeah, this is my pet dread cloud.
I might.
I could take dying of frostbite and poisoned tin cans of food. I mean, since existence is a pre-existing condition.
Doomed Holidays to you all.
I would like to be eaten by the Spider that devours the world.
Just aim for a part that kills, I won't have insurance to cover anything superficial.
@kaeldra I feel really gets the mood I'm in today. Also this meme is about poetry, so I'm tagging her
It's wild how badly translated Rumi actually is. Good lord.
Mood.
I can't afford hyperfixation in my budget anymore. I'll only have about $200 left to eat on, and no money for clothes, gas, and hobbies. All of my safe foods i enjoy are jumping up in price. Looks like it's going to be rice and Ramen for the rest of my life starting in January. Horray!
Honestly, posting memes is about the only thing keeping me going right now. Because I know there are many of you who do really see these posts as the highlight of your day. I can't let those people down.
Pippin tax.
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Heard you fell again. Soon Mitch.... Soon.
item.dc:creator: P00pdalla

item.title: Looking for some guidance and support
item.description: My husband and I made the choice that we aren't going to have kids. We're happy with that choice - being parents isn't for us, we don't want the burden of caring for another human, and we're happy as we are. But I've had three friends announce pregnancies in the last week and I'm struggling. Why do I feel abandoned and guilty? I desperately want to be happy for my friends and I just feel immense sadness and... almost grief? I don't really have any friends to talk to about this anymore because, well, they're all parents or soon-to-be parents. I feel so alone.
item.dc:creator: crowoverlord

item.description: Just make your own coffee at home, ya dingus. For your coffee...
